snippets.text: The current study examined the relationship between Chinese consumers' personal value orientations and their Internet usage and online shopping behaviour. The study was based on responses from a sample of 1620 respondents in five cities in China. Based on Schwartz's value structure, four value orientations were identified and named as 'openness to change orientation', 'self- enhancement orientation', 'conservation orientation' and 'self- transcendence orientation.' Logistic regression analysis was used to investigate the relationship between respondents' demographics, socio- economic characteristics, value orientations and their Internet usage and online shopping behaviour. Also, a structural equation modelling analysis was employed. 'Openness to change orientation' and 'self- enhancement orientation' were found to have direct or indirect positive effect on respondents' online shopping behaviour; whereas 'conservation orientation' and 'self- transcendence orientation' were not significant in the equation estimation.

snippets.text: ## Children Born in July and August: A study on the age regulation of primary school admission and student's education access and development
snippets.subject: Article title in Sociological Studies
snippets.reasoning: Title of Liu's article published in a top-tier Chinese sociology journal.

snippets.text: Abstract: This paper discusses at the micro level the educational inequality resulted from the cutoff birthdate regulation in the current primary school admission system. According to our research, among the students who enroll in school, students born in July and August display relative disadvantage and adaptive difficulties in the construction of self- identity and study ability when compared with those born in September and October. Students born between November and June in the following year have medium performance. Moreover, this effect is accumulative. With regard to acquiring education resources, students born in July and August show the lowest possibility of getting into the key senior middle schools, which is \(16.7\% - 22.8\%\) lower than the rest of the student population, while their possibility to get into vocational school is the highest, \(54.2\% - 60.8\%\) higher than the others. This disadvantage is more prominent among the male students. Our study also show that the effect above is very strong and cannot be effectively suppressed by social economic factors. There is an insurmountable “July/August pitfall”.
snippets.subject: Abstract describing research findings on educational inequality
snippets.reasoning: Abstract demonstrates Liu's original quantitative research on educational inequality.

snippets.subject: Dehuan Liu

snippets.text: The age variable is an indispensable variable in the data analysis of social research, and probably all researchers will not lose this variable in the data analysis of social research, but few papers and monographs are devoted to the study of the age variable. How to analyze the age variable, especially how to find the support of previous research results and related theories based on the data analysis, is a very important but often neglected issue to improve the data analysis. There have been some explorations in this area in foreign countries, but they are all about specific models, and there is no comprehensive work on this topic. The book "Age Theory" fills this gap.
snippets.subject: Dehuan Liu
snippets.reasoning: Establishes that Liu's Age Theory fills a gap in both domestic and international scholarship on age variable analysis.

snippets.text: Since the book is focused on the theoretical significance of a time variable like age in social space and the method of data analysis, it has become one of the reference books for research and analysis methods in communication and other disciplines in China upon its publication, and the social response has been excellent. So far, the book has become one of the required readings assigned by Prof. Huixin Ke and Prof. Hao Shen in the Communication Research Methods class at the Communication University of China, as well as in the Media Analysis and Advanced Statistics classes at the School of Journalism and Communication of Peking University. In addition, the content of this book has been applied in teaching, which had demonstrated excellent teaching results. With the application of the ideas of the model in Age Theory, the papers of master students Hui Zhang, Beibei Zhang and Lili Jiang won the honor of Excellent Master Papers, the papers of Jingjin Jiang, Shengxi Chen and Lingfei Wu won the third prize, third prize and excellent prize in the Procter & Gamble Market Research Paper Award, respectively, and the papers of Jing Jing, Tian Tian and other students won the first prize in Challenge Cup of Peking University. In 2015, Professor Dehuan Liu published an article titled "Children Born in July and August: A Study on the Age Regulation of Primary School Admission and Student's Education Access and Development" in Sociological Studies which attracted intensive attraction from the academic circle, society and government. The article put forward a theoretical model for "July and August trap" based on empirical study for the first time and triggered heated discussion on "cut- off birthday for primary school admission" in China. Professor Xie Yu from Princeton University and many other scholars were inspired by the article and further analyzed educational equality. Guided and promoted by Professor Dehuan Liu, the Ministry of Education printed and issued the Notice of the General Office of the Ministry of Education on Enrollment of Students for Compulsory Education in 2017 which clearly stated that "the cut- off birthday for the first grade of primary school shall be specified in accordance with legal regulations and determined based on actual conditions by the provincial education and administration authorities", completely changing the hard- and- fast rule for cut- off birthday and making a great contribution to promoting educational equality in China.

snippets.text: With respect to public health, Professor Dehuan Liu took scholars and graduate students to a village in central China in 2007 which was overwhelmed by AIDS (there were more than 2,000 people in the village. 13 people had died of AIDS. 16 people had been attacked by HIV and more than 30 people were infected with HIV. AIDS was spreading rapidly, but the villagers were unaware of the risk). They conducted in- depth interviews with the villagers to understand their understanding of AIDS, preventative methods and preferences for the methods used to publicize information about and prevent AIDS. After returning to Beijing, Professor Dehuan Liu and his team developed a series of research reports on AIDS prevention in rural areas of China based on the results obtained from that village.
